data.title: AES Corp. gains amid renewed takeover speculation
data.description:
• AES Corp. (AES) ticked higher by 1.3% amid some renewed takeover speculation for the power producer. 
  • Talks between AES (AES) and its potential suitors may have advanced, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Wednesday. It's not clear which company has advanced talks with AES.
  • There were reports last year that BlackRock's (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners LP and Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) were both interested in buying AES (AES).

data.title: National Bank Holdings outlines 10% loan growth and $4 EPS target for 2027 as Vista integration advances
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: National Bank Holdings Corporation (NBHC) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Tim Laney welcomed Vista teammates, highlighting the swift closure of the Vista Bank acquisition and emphasizing, "While the fourth quarter was noisy, we ended the year having grown tangible book per share by 10%, and we grew our CET1 capital ratio to 14.89%." He stated, "Our goal was to enter 2026 with a clean slate and with a focus on profitable growth."
  • Laney praised the completion of Phase 1 of the 2UniFi build, noting a strong net interest income margin of 3.97% to close the year.
  • CFO Nicole Van Denabeele described 2025 as a year where "we executed on key strategic priorities," specifically referencing the Vista acquisition, tangible book value growth, and a full-year net interest margin of 3.94%. She reported, "Excluding onetime items, fourth quarter net income totaled $22.7 million or $0.60 of earnings per diluted share."
  • Van Denabeele explained, "We kick off the year with a combined loan portfolio of approximately $9.4 billion and are projecting 2026 loan growth to be approximately 10%."
  • John Steinmetz, representing Vista, expressed, "We have partnered with a dynamic, high-performing team and platform, and I believe there is a tremendous potential for our combined organization."
  • President Aldis Birkans highlighted, "We originated $591 million in total loans, the second highest loan production quarter in our company's history. $429 million of that came from commercial loan originations, a new record for us."

Outlook

  • NBHC projects 2026 loan growth of approximately 10%, with combined loan portfolio starting at $9.4 billion.
  • Management expects the combined bank to generate earning asset growth of 7% to 10% during 2026.
  • The company projects a fully taxable equivalent net interest margin for 2026 to "remain right around 4%, excluding the impact of future rate moves."
  • For 2026, noninterest income is projected in the range of $75 million to $80 million, and noninterest expense between $320 million and $330 million, with the first half expected at $165 million to $170 million.
  • The company expects to deliver "earnings in excess of $1 per share in the fourth quarter of 2026, which sets the stage for full year earnings exceeding $4 per share in 2027."
  • 2UniFi revenue contribution for 2026 is expected to be $2 million to $4 million, with expenses held flat at $22 million.
  • Effective tax rate for 2026 is projected at approximately 20%.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter net income, excluding onetime items, was $22.7 million or $0.60 per diluted share.
  • On an adjusted basis, full year 2025 net income was $117.6 million or $3.06 per diluted share.
  • Return on tangible assets was 1.3% and return on tangible common equity was 12.2% for 2025.
  • Fourth quarter noninterest income was $14.4 million, including $3.3 million in pretax securities losses.
  • Fourth quarter noninterest expense totaled $72.4 million, including $5.4 million of acquisition costs.
  • Allowance to total loans ratio ended the year at 1.18%.
  • CET1 ratio ended the year at 14.9%.

Q&A

  • Jeff Rulis, D.A. Davidson: Confirmed 10% loan growth, margin near 4%, earnings over $1 in Q4 2026, and $4 in 2027; Nicole Van Denabeele: "That is correct."
  • Rulis: Asked about 2UniFi revenue and costs; Van Denabeele: "$2 million to $4 million 2UniFi revenue projection for 2026, and we will be holding 2UniFi expense flat in 2026, consistent with 2025, which was $22 million."
  • Rulis: Inquired about potential partnership to reduce 2UniFi costs; Tim Laney: "We are very focused on working to establish a partnership that frankly could have the effect, amongst other options, of moving this off the NBH financials altogether..."
  • Kelly Motta, KBW: Asked about growth drivers for 2026; Aldis Birkans and John Steinmetz pointed to contributions from all markets, with strong momentum in commercial loans and expansion in Texas and resort markets.
  • Motta: Questioned margin dynamics and deposit repricing; Van Denabeele: December margin was "a strong 3.97%... There wasn't any interest reversals in the fourth quarter, and the loans that we worked through were already on nonaccrual."
  • Andrew Terrell, Stephens: Asked about partnership timing for 2UniFi; Laney acknowledged previous optimism was premature but emphasized focus on "bringing the right partnership together and moving 2UniFi ahead."
  • Brett Rabatin, Hovde Group: Asked about payoff activity and outlook for net loan growth; Laney and Birkans described diminished scheduled maturities and a risk-off stance shifting to higher confidence for 2026.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed management for specifics on growth, margin, and 2UniFi partnership timing, showing a cautiously optimistic but probing tone.
  • Management presented a confident outlook, with Van Denabeele and Laney repeatedly affirming guidance and strategic direction, using phrases like "we are confident" and "we are well positioned."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone is more assertive about growth and integration benefits, while analysts maintain a consistent focus on execution and future performance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 is now explicit, with 10% loan growth and full-year earnings targets, compared to more cautious or preliminary statements in Q3.
  • Strategic focus has shifted from announcing the Vista acquisition to emphasizing its integration and the benefits for growth, especially in Texas and resort markets.
  • Net interest margin guidance remains in the mid-3.9% to 4% range, with management addressing margin drivers more directly than in Q3.
  • Expense guidance is more detailed, reflecting Vista integration and 2UniFi's shift to operating phase.
  • Analysts' questions continue to focus on growth, margin sustainability, and technology investments, but with greater attention to integration execution and profitability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited elevated provision expense and onetime acquisition costs as fourth-quarter headwinds.
  • The sale of investment securities to avoid surpassing $10 billion in assets was executed to preserve interchange income, but resulted in a $2.6 million after-tax loss.
  • Problem loans were proactively addressed, with $9.1 million in provision expense; Laney stated, "The decision -- we believe a prudent decision was to address these as aggressively as we could in '25 and have a clean runway for '26."
  • Analysts voiced concerns about loan payoffs, credit quality, and the impact of private credit competition, but management expressed confidence in improved asset quality and diminished headwinds for 2026.

Final Takeaway

National Bank Holdings Corporation closed 2025 with the successful integration of Vista Bank, a robust capital position, and a clear focus on profitable growth for 2026. Management projects strong momentum with 10% loan growth, a stable 4% net interest margin, and the launch of 2UniFi's next phase. The company targets over $1 per share in earnings for the fourth quarter of 2026, setting the stage for full-year earnings above $4 per share in 2027, while proactively managing credit, integration costs, and operational efficiency.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: PPG targets mid-single-digit EPS growth in 2026 amid aerospace momentum and operational cost reductions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: PPG Industries (PPG) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Timothy Knavish reported that “2025 was a year of solid commercial, operational, innovation and financial performance for PPG,” highlighting consistent organic growth and strong cash flow, despite a dynamic macroeconomic environment. Knavish emphasized that net sales for the year reached $15.9 billion with 2% organic growth, and adjusted earnings per share were $7.58.
  • Knavish noted, “We achieved record aerospace coating sales and earnings led by strong demand for our technology advanced products,” and cited a 6% increase in Auto OEM net sales, which outpaced the industry. He also pointed to high single-digit organic sales growth in Architectural Coatings in Latin America and broad-based positive sales volume across all regions.
  • Knavish shared that “our segment EBITDA margin for the year was a healthy 19%,” and cash from operations grew to $1.9 billion, enabling $1.4 billion to be returned to shareholders.
  • Knavish addressed capital deployment, stating, “We ended the year with a strong cash balance of $2.2 billion and a net debt position of $5.1 billion, with $700 million of debt maturing in the first quarter of 2026, which we intend to pay from our current cash position.”
  • Senior VP & CFO Vincent Morales stated, “our segment earnings did grow in the fourth quarter. So they were up about $20 million year-over-year.”

Outlook

  • Knavish provided 2026 guidance, anticipating “organic sales growth in the range of flat to positive low single-digit percentage” and “earnings per share growth that at the midpoint of our guidance represents a mid-single-digit percentage increase.” He added, “We expect earnings per share to be flat to growth of low single-digit percentage during the first half of the year and increasing to high single-digit percentage in the second half of the year.”
  • Management expects positive pricing in most performance and architectural businesses, with some offset from modest price declines in the industrial segment. Knavish commented, “You should expect to see positive price in virtually all of the protective businesses... and the Architectural businesses.”
  • Raw material costs are expected to be flat for the year, with some inflation in epoxies, specialty pigments, and metal packaging offset by softness in TiO2 and solvents.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter net sales were $3.9 billion, up 5% year-over-year with 3% organic growth. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $1.51. Segment EBITDA margin for the quarter was 18%.
  • Global Architectural Coatings net sales rose 8% to $951 million, driven by Mexico’s retail performance and project-related recovery. Segment income increased 16% and EBITDA margins improved by nearly 100 basis points.
  • Performance Coatings delivered 5% net sales growth to $1.3 billion, led by double-digit organic growth in aerospace and gains in Protective & Marine Coatings, offset by high single-digit declines in Automotive Refinish organic sales.
  • Industrial Coatings net sales grew 3% to $1.6 billion, with 5% sales volume growth propelled by share gains, and EBITDA margin improved to 15.1%.
  • Operating cash flow for the year was $1.9 billion, supporting share repurchases of $790 million and dividends of $630 million.

Q&A

  • Christopher Parkinson, Wolfe Research: Asked about the drivers of Q4 growth and 2026 expectations. Knavish: “The high-level answer is macro is not better than we expect…there’s a few markets where we’re getting macro help. Most of our businesses were getting share gain help. And…a lot of the share that we’re gaining in packaging is technology driven.”
  • Aleksey Yefremov, KeyBanc: Requested details on organic growth drivers for 2026. Knavish explained positive price trends in most segments and volume growth in aerospace, PMC, packaging, and auto OEM.
  • Kevin McCarthy, Vertical Research: Questioned flat EBITDA despite organic sales growth. Knavish cited “Refinish destocking” as the main drag, with improvement expected in the second half of 2026. Morales added, “our segment earnings did grow in the fourth quarter…up about $20 million year-over-year.”
  • Vincent Andrews, Morgan Stanley: Asked for updates on Refinish. Knavish reiterated confidence in market share momentum, with recovery expected in the second half, supported by new digital and AI tools.
  • David Begleiter, Deutsche Bank: Inquired about aerospace growth and capacity. Knavish: “The growth rate for 2025 was double digit…we’re guiding high single digits for 2026 for Aerospace. We are capacity constrained.”
  • Multiple analysts probed on capital allocation, corporate costs, and M&A; Knavish and Morales highlighted continued buybacks, disciplined capital investment, and prioritization of organic growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts’ tone reflected cautious optimism but focused on macro uncertainty, operating leverage challenges, and the impact of Refinish destocking. Questions repeatedly pressed for clarity on recovery timing and margin progression.
  • Management maintained confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing share gains, innovation, and disciplined cost control. In Q&A, Knavish acknowledged ongoing challenges but reinforced, “We’re confident in our strategy and the strength of our business model to deliver sustainable long-term growth.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment remained slightly negative regarding near-term earnings leverage, while management’s tone was steady and focused on execution and operational resilience.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to a more cautious outlook for the first half of 2026, with explicit expectation of “flat to low single-digit EPS growth” before improving in the second half. This contrasts with the prior quarter’s outlook, which anticipated organic sales and earnings growth but flagged transitory headwinds.
  • Strategic focus remains on organic growth, operational efficiency, and innovation, particularly in aerospace and digital/AI development. Portfolio pruning and cash deployment priorities are consistent.
  • Analysts maintained focus on Refinish normalization, aerospace capacity, and regional performance trends, echoing concerns seen last quarter but seeking more granular recovery signals.
  • Key metric changes include a rise in cash from operations, improved segment EBITDA margins, and continued share repurchases.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited expected softness in global industrial and automotive demand affecting Q1 2026 organic sales and margins.
  • Refinish destocking and normalization of distributor buying patterns are identified as major drags in the first half of 2026, with recovery expected in the second half.
  • Exposure to tariffs, especially in epoxies and specialty pigments, and potential volatility in project-related sales in Mexico and Europe are noted.
  • Analysts highlighted the challenge of achieving operating leverage given margin pressure and corporate cost inflation.

Final Takeaway

PPG’s management highlighted the company’s resilience and multi-engine growth strategy, with 2025 marked by record aerospace sales, strong cash generation, and steady share gains. The outlook for 2026 anticipates muted first-half performance driven by continued Refinish destocking and macro headwinds, but expects volume and margin expansion in the second half, supported by ongoing operational improvements, cost discipline, and innovation in high-growth segments. The management reiterated confidence in delivering sustainable growth and disciplined capital deployment, underpinned by a strong balance sheet and focused execution.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Badger Meter outlines 5-year high single-digit sales growth outlook as PRASA project ramps up
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Badger Meter (BMI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Kenneth Bockhorst, Chairman, President & CEO, stated the company "delivered solid financial results in the fourth quarter, capping off another full year of record sales, profitability and cash flow." He highlighted robust demand for the cellular AMI solution and the integration of SmartCover into the BlueEdge suite of smart water management solutions. Bockhorst also noted the recent award for the PRASA AMI project in Puerto Rico as positioning the company well for long-term growth.
  • Daniel Weltzien, newly appointed VP, CFO & Treasurer, said, "Total sales of $221 million in the fourth quarter of 2025 represented an increase of 8% year-over-year or 2% base sales growth." Weltzien added, "Operating profit margins increased 40 basis points from 19.1% to 19.5%. Base operating earnings increased 9% year-over-year, expanding base operating profit margins by 140 basis points to 20.5%. Gross margins expanded 180 basis points to 42.1% in the fourth quarter from 40.3% in the prior year quarter."
  • Robert Wrocklage, Executive Vice President of North America Municipal Utility, described the PRASA AMI project as "one of the largest deployments in the world," involving 1.6 million service connections and utilizing E-Series ultrasonic meters, ORION Cellular AMI radios, and BEACON SaaS. He emphasized that Badger Meter's role will be supply only and that production will be based in Racine, Wisconsin.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated a high single-digit sales growth outlook over the next five years. Bockhorst stated, "We expect this project pacing dynamic to extend throughout the first half of 2026 until several awarded projects, including PRASA, begin multiyear turnkey deployments."
  • The company does not plan to size the revenue impact of the PRASA project for 2026 or beyond, due to variables affecting deployment timing. Bockhorst emphasized, "We are confident... to generate high single-digit sales growth, operating profit margin expansion and free cash flow conversion in excess of earnings over a 5-year forward time horizon."

Financial Results

  • The company reported total sales of $221 million for Q4 2025, up 8% year-over-year. Utility water product line sales increased 9% year-over-year. Operating earnings grew 10% year-over-year. EPS was $1.14 versus $1.04 in the prior year quarter.
  • Gross margins reached 42.1% in Q4 2025, compared to 40.3% in the previous year. Quarterly free cash flow was $50.8 million, an increase of approximately $3.4 million year-over-year.
  • Software revenue, which includes SmartCover, now exceeds $74 million and represents 8% of total sales. SmartCover delivered $40 million in sales in 2025.

Q&A

  • Robert Mason, Robert W. Baird: Asked about project timing and whether the full impact of project conclusions was seen in Q4. Bockhorst replied, "The first half started out with a higher growth rate, the second half of the year with a lower growth rate. I think the way we see '26 is a lower growth rate in the first half and a higher growth rate in the second half."
  • Nathan Jones, Stifel: Sought clarity on the size and relevance of the PRASA project. Bockhorst explained that PRASA is equivalent to "8 Orlandos" in scale and is highlighted for its scope.
  • Jones also inquired about gross margins. Weltzien responded that structural mix will continue to impact margins positively in 2026, while noting that commodity input costs and tariffs remain areas to monitor.
  • Jeffrey Reive, RBC: Asked about the timing and phasing of large AMI projects. Bockhorst said project ramp-ups are inherently variable and typically expected to unfold over a five-year horizon.
  • Andrew Krill, Deutsche Bank: Questioned sales outlook versus competitors. Bockhorst stated, "We feel confident sales should grow this year even if they're below that high single-digit through the cycle target."
  • Scott Graham, Seaport: Asked about the use of capital allocation to support growth and returns. Bockhorst asserted, "We would never do any shortsighted cost-cutting type things to try to short circuit a result."
  • Michael Fairbanks, JPMorgan: Asked about SmartCover growth prospects. Bockhorst noted, "We fully, fully expect to continue to grow our sewer line monitoring at a higher rate than average."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on project timing, margin sustainability, and the scale of new wins, with a neutral to slightly positive tone as questions sought clarification on timing and strategic growth rather than expressing skepticism.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, frequently referencing visibility into awarded projects and reiterating multi-year growth convictions. Phrases such as "I'm absolutely confident" and "our 5-year conviction is as strong as it's ever been" underscored this stance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts maintained a similarly constructive and forward-looking tone, with management expressing slightly more confidence in awarded project pipelines and integration success.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for high single-digit sales growth over five years remains unchanged, but management provided more detail on project pacing, indicating a lower growth rate in the first half of 2026 and a higher rate in the second half, which is a shift from the previous quarter's higher first-half growth.
  • The PRASA project was discussed in greater depth, highlighting its scale and expected impact on the second half of 2026 and beyond.
  • Management tone remains confident, with increased emphasis on the recurring nature of project revenue and the durability of secular growth drivers.
  • SmartCover's integration progress and profitability improved, with software revenue and SaaS attachment highlighted as growing contributors.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing global tariff and trade uncertainties as a potential headwind for 2026, along with elevated copper and other component prices as a gross margin headwind.
  • There is acknowledged variability in project timing, with awarded projects sometimes sliding by a quarter or two. Weltzien noted, "Each one of these projects is different. Every customer is different."
  • The PRASA project's variables, including deployment pace and funding, could influence year-to-year revenue contributions.

Final Takeaway

Management reiterated its confidence in the company's ability to deliver high single-digit sales growth, operating margin expansion, and strong cash flow conversion over the next five years, supported by robust demand for smart water management solutions and a deep pipeline of awarded and prospective projects. The scale and timing of the PRASA project, continued SmartCover integration, and focus on capital allocation for strategic growth remain central themes for value creation in 2026 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Logitech falls despite strong Q3 while Wedbush maintains bullish views
data.description:
Shares of Logitech (LOGI) fell about 7% on Wednesday despite fiscal third quarter results beating estimates.

Wedbush kept its Outperform rating and $135 price target on Logitech's stock. 

"Logitech has skillfully managed significant margin pressure through product innovation, cost reductions, targeted promotional activity, and supply chain adjustments, leading to gross and operating margin expansion YoY in FQ3. Meanwhile, Logitech has maintained focus on its core business initiatives – expanding B2B, market share in China, reaccelerating video conferencing, and leading in personal workspace solutions (“PWS”) across its markets," said analysts led by Alicia Reese.

The analysts noted that despite the focus on depressed PC shipments in calendar 2026 and potentially beyond due to high memory and other PC component costs, Logitech CEO Hanneke Faber shifted the focus to Logitech’s much larger opportunity to expand attach rates on theexisting PC base.

The analysts added that of the 1.5B to 2B PCs already owned and in use today, fewer than half have an upgraded mouse, and fewer than one-third have an upgraded  keyboard. This indicates the massive white space Logitech can tap into by increasing its marketing initiatives in this category where it has a dominant global market share.

"Logitech's impressive product updates across categories, effective inventory management, and a revitalized Chinese market keep us optimistic as the company plans for FY:27. Driven by its early rebound in video collaboration, expansion into new end markets, successful reinvigoration in China, and PWS strength, Logitech now has $12 per share in cash and has no debt, positioning it to accelerate growth through small tuck-in M&A, facilitate share repurchases, and expand its dividend," said Reese and her team.

The analysts noted that Logitech’s fiscal third quarter revenue of $1.422B exceeded their estimate of $1.415B and the consensus estimate of $1.406B.

Reese and her team noted that the fiscal fourth quarter outlook was above consensus with revenue guided between $1.07B and $1.09B (versus consensus of $1.08B) and operating income between $155M and $165M (versus consensus of $161M and our prior estimate of $175M).

data.title: Coinbase Global shares fall for seven consecutive sessions; at high risk of bad performance
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) shares clocked seven straight sessions of losses on Wednesday, as the stock was 1.4% lower at $207.97.

The largest U.S. crypto platform lost over 7% in the preceding six sessions. Overall, the stock has fallen 7.3% so far this year, compared to the near 2% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index (SP500).

COIN is down 12% over the past one month.

Most recently, Coinbase Global chief executive Brian Armstrong urged lawmakers to protect his crypto exchange's ability to offer reward on stablecoins, as part of his opposition to the Senate Banking Committee's crypto market structure draft bill.

“The CLARITY Act threatens COIN’s ability to pay interest on stablecoin deposits, undermining a key future revenue stream,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Crypto platform rewards are significantly above typical bank deposit rates, with banking groups warning that high-yield crypto tokens could drain deposits and threaten lenders. 

Coinbase Global has characteristics which have been historically associated with poor future stock performance. COIN has decelerating momentum and is overpriced when compared to other financials stocks, to the point that it gets a Sell rating from Seeking Alpha's Quant rating system. The company received C in the prospect of profitability, while it received F in the momentum factor.

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

Turing to the Wall Street, 23 analysts have given the stock a Buy or above rating. Eleven analysts gave the stock hold recommendation, while one positioned Strong Sell.

data.title: MKS announces pricing of private offering of €1B of 4.250% senior notes
data.description:
• MKS (MKSI) on Wednesday announced the pricing of its private offering of €1 billion aggregate principal amount of 4.250% senior notes due 2034.
  • The offering is expected to close on February 4, 2026.
  • MKS estimates that the net proceeds from the offering will be approximately €985 million.
  • MKS intends to use the net proceeds from the offering, together with the net proceeds from its partial refinancing of its existing $2.2 billion U.S. dollar tranche B term loan, the net proceeds from refinancing of its existing €587 million euro tranche B term loan and cash on hand, to (i) prepay approximately $1.3 billion of, and refinance in full, the USD Tranche B Term Loan, and (ii) refinance in full the Euro Tranche B Term Loan.
  • MKSI is +3.87% to $238.5.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Super Bowl 60 betting is poised to shatter records despite prediction markets
data.description:
Sportsbooks in the U.S. are forecast to see $1.75B in betting volume on Super Bowl 60 across the 41 states and territories where it is legal. The amount of legal wagers on the Super Bowl is expected to be more than 10% higher than last year's tally, with the addition of Missouri to the betting mix a positive factor, according to analysis from Legal Sports Report and the Sports Betting Alliance.

As for the Big Game, the Seattle Seahawks will play the New England Patriots in a West Coast vs. East Coast duel that is a rematch of Super Bowl XLIX, which had one of the most famous endings in Super Bowl history with a goal line interception in the last minute. Both teams have strong fan bases, which is expected to boost betting interest.

"This year’s big game will continue the streak of record Super Bowl handle for the regulated U.S. market," highlighted Legal Sports Report analyst Eric Ramsey. "Missouri’s recent launch pads an already favorable outlook for a weekend that serves as the single biggest opportunity of the year for legal sportsbooks and bettors alike," he noted.

Notably, the Super Bowl will be played in Santa Clara, California, where sports betting is not legal. However, this year the growth of prediction market platforms means there are more ways for bettors to participate. Platforms currently offering Super Bowl 60 prediction market contracts in certain states include Kalshi (KALSHI), Polymarket (POLYMARKET), DraftKings Predictions (DKNG), FanDuel Predicts (FLUT), Robinhood (HOOD), Uderdog, Webull (BULL), and Gemini (GEMI).

data.title: Paramount is planning a serious move into short-form video — Business Insider
data.description:
Paramount Skydance Corp. (PSKY) is planning to add short-form video to its Paramount+ streaming service, according to a report by Business Insider on Wednesday, citing internal presentations and emails.

The report said Dan Reich, the head of global product and design for Paramount+, emailed fellow executives in mid-January, asking to set up a meeting with the company's product chief Dane Glasgow to discuss "Short Form Clips."

"We are trying to figure out how to jump-start efforts to get a million clips into our Short Form UX as quickly as possible," Reich reportedly said in an email, which was seen by the news outlet. 

Short-form video is a top priority for Paramount+ in the first quarter, specifically in its mobile app, according to an internal presentation sent by Reich that was viewed by Business Insider.

The documents viewed by Business Insider suggest that the short-form clips on Paramount+ will initially be created from existing content.

However, the David Ellison-run media company is also interested in hosting user-generated content ((UGC)) like YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL) and TikTok (TIKTOK) (BDNCE). 

A person familiar with Paramount's short-form video plans told Business Insider that the appeal of UGC is that it could be "cheap and brings eyes to Paramount."

Another person familiar with Paramount's thinking said there are many products and initiatives in testing on the streaming side, and that the results of those tests would determine leadership's priorities.

data.title: General Dynamics outlines $54.3B–$54.8B revenue target for 2026 while advancing record backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: General Dynamics Corporation (GD) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Chairperson & CEO Phebe Novakovic highlighted fourth quarter earnings of $4.17 per diluted share on revenue of $14.379 billion, operating earnings of $1.452 billion, and net earnings of $1.143 billion. Novakovic stated that “revenue is up 7.8% and operating earnings are up 2%” year-over-year, emphasizing the strong sequential gains across all financial measures and noting, “the sequential comparisons are quite attractive.” She also underscored that “both revenue and operating earnings were up for each of the segments, led by Marine Systems and Aerospace.”
  • Novakovic described Aerospace as having “continuing strong demand for Gulfstream aircraft, overall strength in Gulfstream service business and continued growth and performance improvement at Jet Aviation.” She reported the delivery of 158 new aircraft, with the G700 and G800 models driving increased demand. On the defense side, Novakovic cited “robust order intake” in Combat Systems, particularly in munitions and European Land Systems, and described Marine Systems as experiencing “exceptional revenue growth,” with a focus on submarine programs and Electric Boat.
  • President Danny Deep explained the $104 million decrease in Aerospace operating earnings was mainly due to three fewer G600 deliveries, lower favorable settlements, higher overhead, and the imposition of tariffs. However, Deep noted, “earnings for the G800 more than replaced the G650 earnings … The G700 also experienced higher earnings despite 2 fewer deliveries.”
  • Deep provided details on key Combat Systems awards, including “over $4 billion for our EAGLE tactical vehicles” in Germany, $600 million in Norway and the UK for bridges, and $640 million in Canada for light armored and logistics vehicles.
  • Senior VP & CFO Kimberly Kuryea said, “Our order activity and backlog continued to be a strong story and a highlight for us in 2025,” reporting a company-wide book-to-bill ratio of 1.5:1 for the year and a record total backlog of $118 billion, a 30% increase over last year. Kuryea also noted, “free cash flow for the year was just shy of $4 billion for a cash conversion rate of 94%.”

Outlook

  • Novakovic projected 2026 Aerospace revenue of about $13.6 billion, operating margin around 14%, and Gulfstream deliveries of 160. For Combat Systems, expected revenue is between $9.6 billion and $9.7 billion with an operating margin of 14.1%. Marine Group revenue is anticipated in the $17.3 billion to $17.7 billion range, with a 30 basis point operating margin improvement. Technologies revenue is expected to reach up to $13.8 billion, with a slight decrease in operating margin.
  • Company-wide, 2026 revenue is forecasted in the range of $54.3 billion to $54.8 billion, with anticipated operating margins of 10.4% and operating earnings around $5.7 billion. EPS guidance is set between $16.10 and $16.20. Novakovic stated, “we feel very good about our business and the prospects for the year.”

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $14.379 billion, with operating earnings of $1.452 billion and net earnings of $1.143 billion. Aerospace segment revenue was $3.788 billion with earnings of $481 million. Combat Systems revenue reached $2.5 billion, up 5.8% year-over-year, with earnings of $381 million. Marine Systems reported revenue of $4.8 billion and operating earnings of $345 million, representing a 21.7% and 72.5% increase, respectively, over the year-ago quarter. Technologies revenue stood at $3.24 billion with operating earnings of $290 million.
  • Year-end backlog hit $118 billion, while company operating cash flow for 2025 totaled $5.1 billion. Capital expenditures were $1.2 billion for the year. Free cash flow for 2025 was “just shy of $4 billion.”

Q&A

  • Seth Seifman, JPMorgan: Asked about Aerospace margins and supply chain impacts. Danny Deep responded that margins are expected to improve through “improved pricing, improved efficiency, some lower overheads and some lower research and development costs.” He noted headwinds from tariffs and supply chain cost increases.
  • Douglas Harned, Bernstein: Inquired about Marine throughput and supply chain constraints. Novakovic explained, “The supply chain remains the gating item … but once they do, that will improve … our productivity and throughput.”
  • Gautam Khanna, TD Cowen: Sought details on tariff impacts at Gulfstream. Deep said, “The impact of tariffs in 2025 was $41 million … tariffs are contemplated in our 2026 margins.”
  • Scott Deuschle, Deutsche Bank: Questioned free cash flow conversion and Navy support. Kuryea said, “strong operating performance out of the business units … are increasing CapEx to a significant extent, but that's factored in.” Novakovic clarified, “Not at this point” regarding Navy working capital support.
  • Sheila Kahyaoglu, Jefferies: Asked about Gulfstream order drivers and Combat Systems capacity. Novakovic cited new product introductions and strong economies. She also stated, “we have capacity and have executed that capacity up in Northeast Pennsylvania at 36 rounds a month … with the capacity of 50,000 rounds a month.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were broadly positive, focusing on margin expansion, supply chain recovery, and strong order momentum. Multiple analysts probed for details around tariff impacts, throughput improvements, and capital allocation, signaling continued optimism and pressing for confirmation of sustained growth.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, frequently referencing solid operational execution and robust demand. Novakovic repeatedly highlighted, “we feel very good about our business and the prospects for the year.” During the Q&A, management responses remained measured and detailed, with occasional caution expressed around supply chain and tariff headwinds. Compared to the previous quarter, management’s confidence appeared consistent, with a slight increase in emphasis on operational execution and capital investment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance increased from a previous EPS forecast of $15.30–$15.35 to $16.10–$16.20 for 2026, with revenue outlook rising from $52 billion to the $54.3–$54.8 billion range. Management continued to emphasize operational performance and productivity improvements, with a greater focus on investments in Marine Systems and Combat Systems.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted from concerns about government shutdown impacts and supply chain stabilization in Q3 to detailed inquiries on the durability of margin expansion, capital expenditures, and order conversion in Q4.
  • Management tone remained confident, but with more explicit discussion of tariffs, supply chain bottlenecks, and investment priorities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified supply chain constraints as the primary risk to productivity and throughput, particularly in Marine Systems and Aerospace.
  • Tariff impacts on Aerospace were cited as a headwind, with Deep noting higher expected tariffs in 2026.
  • Analysts expressed interest in the sustainability of order momentum, capacity expansion, and the timing of new contracts, especially in the defense segments.
  • Novakovic stated, “The supply chain remains the gating item,” while Deep highlighted the need for continued investments and efficiency gains.

Final Takeaway

General Dynamics concluded the year with strong revenue and earnings growth, record backlog, and robust order activity across all major segments. Management projects continued growth for 2026, led by increased revenue targets and EPS guidance, supported by strategic investments in production capacity and operational improvements, while remaining attentive to risks from supply chain and tariffs. The company maintains a focus on execution and investment to sustain momentum into the coming year.
